criteria.label: Style authority and product context
criteria.pass_evidence: Checks brand authority and the product promise before recommending polish.
criteria.deduction_trigger: Replaces the page with a stock SaaS landing template.

criteria.label: Reference selection
criteria.pass_evidence: Selects visual judgment, principles, design moves, taste, and validation references.
criteria.deduction_trigger: Loads unrelated references or ignores product-taste guidance.

criteria.label: Anti-generic redesign
criteria.pass_evidence: Finds vague copy, equal cards, generic gradients, and missing proof sequence.
criteria.deduction_trigger: Adds decorative effects without improving product specificity.

criteria.label: Evidence level labeling
criteria.pass_evidence: Labels static, browser, responsive, or before/after evidence.
criteria.deduction_trigger: Assigns a high score without evidence level or anti-inflation limits.

criteria.label: Verified/unverified boundary
criteria.pass_evidence: Separates visible hierarchy from unverified conversion and interaction behavior.
criteria.deduction_trigger: Claims conversion, performance, or mobile polish without proof.

criteria.label: Concrete design moves
criteria.pass_evidence: Proposes a specific claim, proof-first hero, varied rhythm, and CTA hierarchy.
criteria.deduction_trigger: Says only to make the page premium without implementable moves.

criteria.label: Scope control and unrelated changes
criteria.pass_evidence: Keeps recommendations within landing UI, copy hierarchy, and evidence.
criteria.deduction_trigger: Suggests unrelated architecture or backend changes.
